The collaboration will focus on advanced marine batteries and electric propulsion systems to support global green shipping requirements. The JV aims to indigenize critical technology, helping the Indian maritime sector adopt sustainable energy solutions.

articles.headline: Five-star hotel at Gandhinagar station reports low 38 percent occupancy
articles.summary: The Leela Gandhinagar, India's first five-star hotel built above a railway station, continues to face low occupancy rates four years after its 2021 opening. In FY25, occupancy averaged just 38%, with gross revenue declining slightly to ₹78.56 crore. The redevelopment project, which cost nearly ₹800 crore, has yet to see strong returns despite its unique infrastructure and prime location.

articles.headline: Noida Airport CEO says Indian aviation demand remains resilient
articles.summary: Christoph Schnellmann, CEO of Noida International Airport, stated that India’s aviation growth remains strong despite supply chain issues and the West Asia crisis. The airport at Jewar is preparing for its inauguration on March 28 by PM Modi. Operational readiness includes security clearances from BCAS and DGCA, with domestic flights expected to take off 30-45 days after the launch.

articles.headline: British Airways increases flight capacity to India through May
articles.summary: British Airways is operating additional daily flights from London Heathrow to Delhi and Mumbai until the end of May to meet strong demand. The expansion provides over 1,000 extra seats per week between India and the UK. The airline will operate 63 weekly flights to India, offering greater flexibility for travelers navigating disruptions in West Asian air corridors.

articles.headline: Air China resumes Beijing-Delhi flights after scheduled service hiatus
articles.summary: Air China will launch thrice-weekly flights between Beijing and Delhi starting April 21 using Airbus A330 aircraft. This marks the fourth airline to resume non-stop connectivity between India and China since last November. The move is expected to improve people-to-people ties and provide onward connections to North America, despite ongoing concerns regarding visa processing timelines.

articles.headline: Centre prepares to extend EV subsidies under new FAME scheme
articles.summary: The Ministry of Heavy Industries is finalizing a proposal to extend subsidies for electric vehicles under a new iteration of the FAME scheme. The initiative aims to support the adoption of electric two-wheelers and buses. The move is designed to maintain the momentum of green mobility and support domestic manufacturing with a projected outlay of approximately ₹10,000 crore.

articles.headline: Multi-layered security and holding areas introduced at Chinnaswamy stadium
articles.summary: The Bengaluru City Police and State Expert Committee have implemented a new security protocol for the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium ahead of the IPL. The venue now features nine holding areas to manage crowds and a three-tier gate system for ticket verification and frisking. Police will deploy 2,000 officers and seven special squads to monitor the area and prevent unauthorized gatherings.

articles.headline: Comprehensive traffic and parking restrictions announced for Bengaluru IPL matches
articles.summary: Bengaluru police have released a traffic advisory for IPL matches on March 28 and April 5. Parking is prohibited on several key roads including Queen’s Road, M.G. Road, and Raj Bhavan Road. Dedicated pick-up points for cabs and autos have been set up at B.R.V. Ground and Manekshaw Parade Grounds. Spectators are urged to use the Namma Metro and BMTC services to avoid congestion.

articles.headline: Tunnel road between Hebbal and Silk Board most viable for Bengaluru
articles.summary: Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar stated that a tunnel road is the only practical solution to Bengaluru's traffic woes, as road widening is too costly. The project is estimated to cost ₹17,000 crore for construction and ₹3,000 crore for land acquisition. Shivakumar noted that the per-kilometer cost of ₹700–800 crore is lower than similar projects in Mumbai or other Indian states.

articles.headline: KIA to discontinue physical disembarkation cards for foreign nationals
articles.summary: Kempegowda International Airport (KIA) issued an advisory stating that physical disembarkation cards for foreign nationals will be discontinued starting April 1, 2026. Travelers are required to complete a digitized e-Arrival card within 72 hours of their arrival. The move is part of an initiative to streamline immigration processes through digital integration.

articles.headline: NHAI invites bids for ₹22.10 crore service road project on Sira bypass
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has invited bids for the construction of 3.23 km of two-lane service roads on the Sira bypass (NH-48). The ₹22.10 crore project aims to segregate local and highway traffic to improve safety and support urban growth in Sira. The work will be executed under the Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) model.

articles.headline: Mumbai local train services disrupted due to overhead wire technical glitch
articles.summary: Suburban train services on Mumbai's Central Line were severely affected on Wednesday morning due to a technical snag in the overhead equipment near Thakurli station. Thousands of commuters were stranded during peak hours before the technical team restored the power supply and resumed limited services.

articles.headline: Passengers slam new rail ticket cancellation policy by Indian Railways
articles.summary: Commuters have criticized a new railway ticket cancellation policy that imposes higher fees for short-notice cancellations. Under the new rules, tickets cancelled within 72-24 hours of departure will incur a 50% deduction, and no refund will be issued for cancellations made less than 8 hours before departure. The policy aims to improve ticket availability.

articles.headline: Karnataka identifies 940 accident blackspots, including 460 on highways
articles.summary: A state survey has identified 940 accident blackspots in Karnataka, with 460 located on national and state highways. Bengaluru city alone has 64 such spots. The 8th Mile junction on Tumakuru Road was named the most dangerous urban spot, recording 40 accidents and 12 deaths. Authorities plan long-term interventions, including pedestrian bridges and wider roads.

articles.headline: Karnataka government plans ordinance to boost city's vertical growth
articles.summary: The Karnataka government intends to bring an ordinance to amend the Karnataka Apartment Ownership and Management Act 2025. The move aims to allow higher Floor Area Ratio (FAR) and promote vertical growth in Bengaluru. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar stated that the policy will help meet the growing demand for housing and ensure better urban planning.

articles.headline: New flyovers and corridors planned to ease Bengaluru's traffic congestion
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has announced plans to build 300 km of new roads, including flyovers and double-decker corridors, to ease traffic in Bengaluru. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar stated that the projects, estimated to cost ₹3,000 crore, will be completed within four years. The initiative also includes the development of storm water drains.

articles.headline: Telcos Seek Fallback Options Amid Subsea Cable Risks
articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications has met with operators to draw up a risk analysis for undersea cables passing through the Strait of Hormuz. About a third of India's westward internet traffic flows through this route. Operators are looking at fallback options and alternative routes, including Pacific and South-East Asian paths, to avoid potential disruptions from regional conflict.

articles.headline: Noida Authority approves ₹25 crore for sound barriers on elevated road
articles.summary: The Noida Authority has approved ₹25 crore to install six-foot-high sound barriers on the Bhangel elevated road. The project follows complaints from residents regarding high noise levels since the road opened in January 2026. The barriers will be installed along a 4.5-km stretch passing through residential areas like Sector 49 and Barola, vetted by IIT Delhi.

articles.headline: DTC bus fleet drops to 13-year low of 3,213 vehicles
articles.summary: The Delhi Transport Corporation’s fleet size has fallen to 3,213 buses, its lowest in 13 years, according to the Economic Survey of Delhi 2025-26. Ridership has also declined by 44% since 2013-14. The drop is linked to the retirement of old CNG buses and slow induction of new electric vehicles, even as e-buses now make up 70% of the total public transport fleet.

articles.headline: Kite string causes flash and train delay at Jafrabad Metro
articles.summary: A kite string stuck in overhead power cables at Jafrabad Metro station caused a loud sound and minor flashing on Thursday. DMRC officials confirmed that trains were temporarily halted and passengers evacuated as a precaution. The incident was not a blast, and normal operations resumed shortly after the string was cleared from the high-voltage lines.

articles.headline: Apprenticeship scheme dropouts increase 15-fold over five years
articles.summary: The number of people dropping out of the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) has surged to 4.25 lakh in FY26, up from 27,183 in FY21. While total enrollments have grown significantly to 12.1 lakh, the completion rate has not kept pace. The dropout rate now stands at approximately 35%, highlighting challenges in industry-based skill training.

articles.headline: Indian Agricultural Research Institute debuts in QS World University Rankings
articles.summary: The Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI), or Pusa Institute, has entered the QS World University Rankings for ‘Agriculture & Forestry’ for the first time. Placed in the 151-200 band, IARI joined other Indian institutions like BHU and IIT-Kharagpur. ICAR Director General ML Jat credited the recognition to high-quality research and skilled human resource development in the sector.

articles.headline: Karnataka announces 60-day age relaxation for Class 1 admissions
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has announced a 60-day relaxation in age eligibility for Class 1 admissions for the 2026-27 academic year. Children who are 5 years and 10 months old by June 1 will now be eligible, easing the previous strict 6-year requirement. The move follows pressure from parents concerned about children losing an academic year due to pandemic-related disruptions.

articles.headline: SSLC third language examination in Karnataka postponed to March 31
articles.summary: The Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB) has moved the SSLC third language exam from March 30 to March 31. The change follows the State government's declaration of a public holiday for Mahaveer Jayanti on March 30. Other examination schedules remain unchanged. Subjects affected include Hindi, Kannada, English, Arabic, and Sanskrit.

articles.headline: CAG report identifies illegal grants to private engineering colleges
articles.summary: The CAG found that the Karnataka government illegally released ₹146.23 crore in grants to BVBCET (Hubballi) and SJCE (Mysuru) after they became private universities. Under their respective Acts, private universities must be self-financing. The audit also noted failures in recovering ₹5.72 crore in salaries paid to faculty who failed to complete PhDs while on government deputation.

articles.headline: KSOU V-C to consult Governor over frozen bank accounts
articles.summary: KSOU Vice-Chancellor Sharanappa V. Halase will consult Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot after the State government ordered banks to freeze the university's accounts. The freeze followed a dispute over KSOU hosting a conference on Hindutva ideology. The V-C warned that the move would hinder salary payments and daily operations, despite a High Court stay on the government's cancellation order.

articles.headline: Karnataka reduces Class 1 admission age by 60 days
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has relaxed the age criteria for admission to Class 1 by 60 days for the academic year 2026-27. A child must be six years old as of June 1 to be eligible for admission. The new rule applies to all government, aided, and private schools across various curricula including State, CBSE, and ICSE.

articles.headline: Professional IGNOU degrees may be rendered invalid due to distance mode
articles.summary: Thousands of students enrolled in professional courses at IGNOU, such as engineering and pharmacy, may find their degrees invalid. The University Grants Commission (UGC) has reiterated that professional courses cannot be offered through distance learning modes. This move follows a national policy to ensure clinical and practical training standards are met.

articles.headline: Ghaziabad cancels order for online classes on airport inauguration day
articles.summary: The Ghaziabad district administration has retracted its order directing schools to hold online classes on March 28. Physical classes will now proceed as scheduled on the day Prime Minister Modi inaugurates the Noida airport. However, the plan to requisition 800 school buses to ferry beneficiaries to the event remains, though the transport department will now manage the arrangement.

articles.headline: Delhi Chief Minister distributes bicycles to 1,100 government school girls
articles.summary: Chief Minister Rekha Gupta distributed bicycles to 1,100 Class 10 girls from government schools in Shalimar Bagh. The initiative aims to support female students' mobility and educational access. During the event in Pitampura, the CM emphasized the government's commitment to empowering young girls through direct support and improving school infrastructure.

articles.headline: War and geopolitical cracks: Impact of Iran conflict on energy
articles.summary: The US-Iran conflict is redefining 21st-century energy geopolitics, with the Strait of Hormuz becoming a strategic weapon. Iran's decision to close the strait has disrupted 20% of global oil and LNG flows. This shift forces nations toward energy diversification and highlights the fragility of global supply chains, while raising costs for international trade and electricity.

articles.headline: Global food crisis possible if West Asia war continues: Matt Simpson
articles.summary: Brazil Potash CEO Matt Simpson warns that if the West Asia conflict persists through April, a global food crisis is likely. The region provides half of the world's phosphate and 10% of its potash. Supply disruptions will force farmers to reduce fertilizer use, leading to lower crop yields and higher food prices in Europe, North America, and India.

articles.headline: Iran allows friendly nations commercial access to Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi announced that India, China, and Russia are permitted to use the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping. The move comes as global oil and gas prices surge after Iran effectively blocked the strategic waterway, which carries 20% of global oil and LNG. Araghchi clarified that ships linked to adversaries remain blocked from the transit lane.

articles.headline: France invites India and other nations to June G7 summit
articles.summary: France will host leaders from India, South Korea, Brazil, and Kenya at the G7 summit in June at Evian-les-Bains. The invitation aims to broaden support for correcting global economic imbalances and preventing a financial crisis. Discussions are expected to address the US deficit, European production, and the impact of the ongoing Iran crisis on global energy and economic stability.

articles.headline: Activists and MEPs call for suspension of Israel’s WTO MFN status
articles.summary: A coalition of 27 civil society groups and European parliamentarians has called for the suspension of Israel’s Most-Favoured-Nation (MFN) status at the WTO. The demand follows allegations of international law violations in the Occupied Palestinian Territory and Gaza. Proponents argue that WTO members should act consistently with the suspension of Russia’s MFN status after its invasion.

articles.headline: Chinese Ambassador says India-China differences are being deliberately amplified
articles.summary: China’s Ambassador to India, Xu Feihong, stated that differences between the two nations are hyped by those seeking to profit from the discord. He urged both sides to carry forward the Eastern wisdom of peaceful coexistence and cooperation within the Global South. Feihong noted that relations have improved following meetings between PM Modi and President Xi Jinping in Kazan.

articles.headline: Global oil prices decline amid uncertainty over US interest rate cuts
articles.summary: International crude oil prices dropped as investors weighed the impact of prolonged high interest rates in the United States. Brent crude fell toward $82 per barrel. Concerns about sluggish demand in major economies offset geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Market participants are looking ahead to the upcoming OPEC+ meeting for signals on production levels for the next quarter.

articles.headline: Iran grants Hormuz passage to India and four other nations
articles.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced that ships from India, China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan are permitted passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran has established sovereignty over the waterway following conflict with the U.S. Araghchi thanked India and Sri Lanka for assisting an Iranian vessel sunk in a U.S. attack and denied holding direct talks with Washington.

articles.headline: Jaishankar discusses Hormuz and West Asia at G7 meeting in France
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar represented India at the G7 Foreign Ministers' meeting in France, focusing on West Asia tensions and the security of the Strait of Hormuz. Jaishankar met with counterparts from France, Canada, Japan, and South Korea. Discussions centered on maritime security, global governance reforms, and energy challenges impacting the Global South.

articles.headline: China expresses willingness to work with India in BRICS and Global South
articles.summary: Following a meeting between Wang Yi and outgoing Ambassador Pradeep Kumar Rawat, China's Foreign Ministry stated it views India as a 'development opportunity' rather than a threat. Beijing seeks to strengthen multilateral collaboration through BRICS and safeguard the interests of the Global South. Chinese Ambassador Xu Feihong also called for a 'Dragon-Elephant Tango' and greater cultural cooperation.

articles.headline: Iran rejects U.S. peace framework as oil prices surge
articles.summary: Iran has rejected a 15-point U.S. 'action list' for a peace deal delivered through Pakistan, as military tensions escalate. Tehran is reportedly planning to charge ships for passage through the Strait of Hormuz, with some vessels already paying in yuan. International Brent crude traded at $104 on Thursday, up 40% since the conflict began, sparking fears of a global energy crisis.

articles.headline: Israel claims killing of Iranian Revolutionary Guards’ navy commander
articles.summary: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced the elimination of Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the Iranian Revolutionary Guards’ navy, in an air strike. Tangsiri was allegedly responsible for mining and blocking the Strait of Hormuz. The strike also killed naval intelligence chief Behnam Rezaei. Israel has expanded its targets to include Iranian naval assets in the Caspian Sea.

articles.headline: Iran begins funeral rites for President Raisi following helicopter crash
articles.summary: Massive crowds gathered in Tabriz and Tehran as Iran began funeral ceremonies for President Ebrahim Raisi, Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian, and others killed in a helicopter crash. The country has entered five days of mourning, with Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei leading the prayers for the deceased leaders.

articles.headline: Singapore Airlines flight turbulence: One dead and dozens injured
articles.summary: A 73-year-old British man died of a suspected heart attack and over 30 people were injured when a Singapore Airlines flight from London hit severe turbulence over the Irrawaddy Basin. The Boeing 777-300ER was forced to make an emergency landing in Bangkok, where passengers were treated for spinal and head injuries.

articles.headline: Iran hands Hormuz pass to India, Pak, Russia, China, and Iraq
articles.summary: Iran has allowed 20 India-flagged vessels to exit the Strait of Hormuz and 18 to enter to load cargo. This follows intense negotiations as Tehran reportedly seeks a deal with the US amidst an oil threat. The move aims to clear a "choke point" for friendly nations. At least 11 LPG tankers are currently waiting to cross the strait.

articles.headline: Israel removes two top Iranian names from hit list
articles.summary: Israel removed Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi and parliamentary speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf from its hit list following a request from Pakistan to the United States. Pakistani officials reportedly urged Washington to press Israel not to target these leaders. The request was made during discussions on regional peace.

articles.headline: Balendra Shah to take oath as Nepal's youngest Prime Minister
articles.summary: Balendra Shah, 35, is set to become Nepal's youngest Prime Minister today. The former rapper and Kathmandu mayor will govern from Singha Durbar, the nation's seat of power. Shah, an structural engineer by training, rose to prominence after winning the mayoral election as an independent candidate.

articles.headline: Zelensky makes surprise visit to Saudi Arabia for security pact
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Saudi Arabia for an unannounced visit to sign a security cooperation pact. The agreement focuses on air protection and military cooperation. Zelensky also stated that the US is linking its security guarantees to Ukraine giving up the Donbas region, as part of a proposed peace deal.

articles.headline: China envoy says India and China are inseparable neighbors
articles.summary: China's newly appointed envoy to India, Xu Feihong, stated that the two nations are "neighbors that can't be parted." Addressing the India-China youth dialogue, he emphasized the need for win-win cooperation and urged both sides to manage differences constructively. Xu remarked that the rise of Asian giants was due to the hard work of their people.

articles.headline: US links Ukraine security guarantees to Donbas territorial control
articles.summary: The United States has reportedly informed Ukraine that future security guarantees are conditional on Kyiv accepting a peace deal that involves giving up the Donbas region. President Zelensky has rejected the proposal, stating that Ukraine will not trade its territory for peace. The US plan aims to bring a swift end to the four-year-old conflict.

articles.headline: Trump Warns Iran to 'Get Serious' About Peace Negotiations
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump warned Iran to "get serious" about peace negotiations or face severe military consequences. Trump claimed that US forces are ready to intervene if Iranian-backed groups continue to threaten regional stability. The statement comes amid reports of secret mediation efforts in Oman aimed at de-escalating the conflict and avoiding a full-scale regional war.

articles.headline: China Seeks Conditions for West Asia Peace Talks
articles.summary: China has called for "sincere" conditions to be met before starting peace talks in West Asia. Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian stated that China is engaging with all parties, including Iran and the US, to find a diplomatic solution. Beijing has expressed concern over regional stability and is positioning itself as a potential mediator, though it remains critical of Western influence.

articles.headline: 26 dead as bus plunges into Padma River in Bangladesh
articles.summary: A bus carrying approximately 50 people plunged into the Padma River in Bangladesh’s Rajbari district while driving onto a ferry. Rescuers have recovered 26 bodies, including eleven women and five children. The accident occurred on Wednesday as many were returning from Eid al-Fitr festivities. Strong currents and heavy rain initially hampered the recovery efforts.

articles.headline: US and Iran at impasse as West Asia conflict escalates
articles.summary: Iran and the US are at a diplomatic deadlock over ceasefire talks. Iranian officials rejected a US proposal as one-sided and serving Israeli interests. President Donald Trump warned Tehran to abandon nuclear ambitions or face a continued onslaught. Meanwhile, Israel claimed to have killed IRGC Navy chief Alireza Tangsiri, who was allegedly leading the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Iran mines Kharg Island as US military presence in Gulf increases
articles.summary: Iran has begun laying mines around its primary oil export hub, Kharg Island, amid reports of increased US troop deployments. The US has reportedly ordered 2,000 more soldiers to the region, joining a presence of 50,000. Iran warned that any ground operation would lead to retaliatory attacks on the vital infrastructure of any regional state supporting an invasion.

articles.headline: Iranian parliament drafting bill to impose fees on Hormuz shipping
articles.summary: The Iranian parliament is working on a law that would impose transit fees on vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The legislation would formally recognize Iran's oversight of the vital waterway. This follows reports that Iran is already seeking informal tolls of up to $2 million from commercial ships to ensure safe passage during the ongoing West Asia conflict.

articles.headline: Ukraine targets Russian energy hubs after US eases oil sanctions
articles.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky stated that Ukraine will continue long-range drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure to maintain pressure as global oil sanctions are eased. Recent strikes on the Ust-Luga and Primorsk terminals have halted approximately 40% of Russia's oil export capacity. Zelensky emphasized that Ukraine must respond to Russian aggression to force a truce.

articles.headline: US warns of potential Houthi attacks in Bab el-Mandeb Strait
articles.summary: The US has warned that Houthi militants may target commercial vessels in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. The alert follows threats from Tehran about expanding shipping barriers. The strait is a key conduit for Saudi Arabian oil flows. While Houthi attacks had slowed during the Gaza ceasefire, US maritime officials now anticipate renewed hostilities as regional tensions rise.

articles.headline: Border fighting between Afghanistan and Pakistan resumes after ceasefire
articles.summary: Fighting has resumed along the Afghan-Pakistan border following the expiration of an Eid ceasefire. Pakistani artillery shells killed two Afghan civilians in Kunar province, prompting retaliatory fire from Afghan Taliban forces. The violence follows a series of cross-border strikes earlier this month, including a Pakistani attack on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul.

articles.headline: Chennai's heritage clubs switch to wood-fire cooking amid LPG shortage
articles.summary: Historic clubs in Chennai, including the Cosmopolitan and Madras Gymkhana, are reverting to wood-fire cooking and electric stoves due to severe LPG shortages. The clubs have removed gas-heavy items like live dosa counters and deep-fried dishes from their menus. Members have reportedly embraced the change, noting that wood-fired dishes offer a traditional taste.

articles.headline: Yamunotri temple opens doors to devotees of all faiths
articles.summary: Authorities at the Yamunotri temple have announced that all devotees are welcome, regardless of their faith. This marks a significant shift for the Himalayan shrine. Non-Hindu entry restrictions have been removed for the upcoming Char Dham yatra, which begins on April 19. The move contrasts with stricter rules at Kedarnath and Gangotri temples.

articles.headline: Odisha government bans liquor shops near Puri’s Jagannath Temple
articles.summary: In a move to respect religious sentiments, the Odisha government has decided that no excise shops will be allowed near the 12th-century Jagannath Temple or along the Grand Road in Puri. The ban is part of a broader effort to maintain the sanctity of the temple's surroundings for pilgrims and residents alike.

articles.headline: Virat Kohli adds plant-based vegan tattoo ahead of IPL 2026
articles.summary: Cricketer Virat Kohli added a new plant-based, cruelty-free tattoo to his left arm ahead of the IPL 2026 season. The tattoo, which took over 24 hours across three sessions, restores a faded Shiva design with collaborative mandala work. Tattoo artist Allan Gois described the project as highly demanding and meaningful, utilizing multiple forms of precision expertise.

articles.headline: Actor Danish Iqbal discusses playing Dawood Ibrahim in ‘Dhurandhar’
articles.summary: Actor Danish Iqbal revealed he did not know his character 'Bade Sahab' in 'Dhurandhar The Revenge' was Dawood Ibrahim until the script unfolded. Iqbal expressed initial anxiety about portraying the strong character. Meanwhile, director Aditya Dhar has threatened legal action against viral AI-generated posters of the film that misrepresented his vision.

articles.headline: Anupam Kher returns to theater with ‘Jaane Pehchaane Anjaane’
articles.summary: Celebrating World Theatre Day, actor Anupam Kher announced his return to the stage with the play 'Jaane Pehchaane Anjaane.' Kher described theater as a 'pure magic' necessity that keeps him alive. The production plans an international tour across the US and Canada, with Kher emphasizing that the emotional appeal of theater remains timeless across cultures.

articles.headline: Delhi University dramatics societies showcase powerful storytelling on World Theatre Day
articles.summary: DU's dramatics societies, including Ramjas’ Shunya and Shivaji’s Vayam, are using theater to address issues like the Yamuna ecological crisis and linguistic chauvinism. These groups have won numerous awards and recently collaborated with ministries for awareness plays. Campus theater culture remains vibrant, with students using the stage to challenge and awaken society.

articles.headline: Actor Pratik Gandhi emphasizes theater’s role in shaping his career
articles.summary: Actor Pratik Gandhi credited theater for his success, despite its challenges as a 'poor-paying medium.' On World Theatre Day, Gandhi highlighted theater as a source of energy and experience, though he noted that infrastructure must improve to support larger audiences. He observed that ticket pricing in the West is significantly higher, reflecting a different economic model for the arts.

articles.headline: Casting of Paapa Essiedu as Severus Snape sparks online controversy
articles.summary: The casting of Black actor Paapa Essiedu as Severus Snape in the upcoming Harry Potter TV series has sparked racial controversy and online trolling. While some fans criticized the departure from the original films, Essiedu revealed he has faced racist death threats. The series, based on JK Rowling's books, is receiving mixed reactions as it begins to reveal its cast.

articles.headline: Iran war threatens battery metal production as sulphur shipments halt
articles.summary: The production of copper, nickel, and cobalt is at risk as sulphur shipments through the Strait of Hormuz have ceased due to the Iran war. Sulphur is essential for producing sulphuric acid used in metal refining. Prices have surged 25% since the conflict began. Research agency BMI warned that a prolonged closure will disrupt supply chains for lithium-ion batteries and other electronics.

articles.headline: India to push for E-100 and higher ethanol blending targets
articles.summary: Food Secretary Sanjeev Chopra announced that India’s ethanol programme will now focus on higher blending targets, flex-fuel vehicles, and E-100 adoption. To ensure feedstock security, the government will redirect 90 lakh tonnes of broken rice from the PDS to distilleries. The move aims to accelerate bio-energy use as global crude oil prices rise due to geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: Elitecon International launches smart farming CSR for tobacco growers
articles.summary: Elitecon International has launched a CSR initiative to introduce precision agriculture to tobacco farmers in Andhra Pradesh. The programme aims to reduce irrigation water usage by 65% and lower energy consumption by 45% using IoT sensors and drone-assisted protection. These technologies are projected to increase crop productivity by 15% and farmer income by ₹1.4 lakh per hectare.

articles.headline: KSPCA urges crackdown on vehicle noise and modified silencers
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Police Complaints Authority has requested strict action against vehicles using illegal modified silencers and shrill horns. Highlighting health risks like hearing impairment and mental stress, the authority recommended special drives by all police stations to seize offending vehicles under the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988, particularly in urban areas like Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Karnataka Forest Minister seeks recovery of forest land funds from IAF
articles.summary: Forest Minister B. Eshwar Khandre informed the Assembly that the Indian Air Force (IAF) is in illegal possession of forest land in Jarakbande Kaval. The government plans to recover compensation the IAF received from BMRCL for forest land used for metro projects. The IAF has been asked to regularize land use under the Forest Conservation Act and provide land for compensatory afforestation.

articles.headline: India must enhance battery storage for non-fossil capacity
articles.summary: India has committed to a 2035 NDC goal of 60% electric capacity from non-fossil sources and a 47% reduction in emissions intensity. While India met its 2030 target early, only 25% of power generated is currently non-fossil due to storage limitations. The editorial argues that achieving net-zero by 2070 requires urgent investment in battery storage and grid improvements to utilize solar and wind power.

articles.headline: Severe heatwave grips Delhi as temperatures soar to 47.4 degrees Celsius
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department issued a red alert for Delhi as Najafgarh recorded a maximum temperature of 47.4 degrees Celsius, the highest in the country. Severe heatwave conditions are expected to persist across North India, including Rajasthan, Punjab, and Haryana, for the next few days.

articles.headline: Gulf energy infrastructure damage estimated at $25 billion
articles.summary: A Rystad Energy assessment shows that strikes on energy infrastructure across six Gulf countries will cost $25 billion to repair. Qatar’s Ras Laffan facility recovery may take 3-5 years, impacting India which gets 40% of its LNG from Qatar. Currently, 54% of India’s LNG imports transit the Strait of Hormuz, where industrial gas rationing is already beginning.

articles.headline: Wetland Authority reports challenges in reviving 1,300 Delhi water bodies
articles.summary: The Delhi State Wetland Authority informed the NGT that reviving many of the city's water bodies is difficult due to extensive encroachment and their use for housing or infrastructure. Data shows a significant portion of identified land is already built-up. The authority is working with land-owning agencies to demarcate sites and conduct water quality assessments.

articles.headline: Delhi forest department to create eight new water bodies in Ridge
articles.summary: Eight new water bodies will be created in the Central and Southern Ridge to improve local ecology and manage waterlogging. The project will use natural slopes and channels to divert monsoon stormwater to low-lying areas. Experts have cautioned that only rainwater, not sewage or treated wastewater, should enter these bodies to avoid negatively impacting the Ridge's flora and fauna.

articles.headline: Indian health insurance premiums exceed ₹1.2 lakh crore in FY25
articles.summary: India’s health insurance sector grew 9% in FY25, with total premiums surpassing ₹1.2 lakh crore. The Finance Ministry attributed the rise to increased awareness and new IRDAI regulations that mandate cashless claim processing within three hours. While 93% of grievances were resolved, many claim rejections are still linked to policy sub-limits and non-medical expense clauses.

articles.headline: Health Ministry issues heatwave advisory as temperatures soar across India
articles.summary: The Union Health Ministry released a comprehensive advisory to states as several regions face extreme heatwave conditions. Guidelines include setting up dedicated heat-stroke clinics in hospitals and ensuring availability of essential medicines and IV fluids. Citizens are advised to stay hydrated and avoid outdoor activities during peak sun hours to prevent heat-related illnesses.

articles.headline: Stem cell therapy for autism declared illegal by NMC
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has issued an advisory declaring stem cell therapy illegal for treating Autism Spectrum Disorder. The decision aligns with a Supreme Court order and ICMR recommendations to curb deceptive practices by private clinics. Stem cell therapy is now restricted to 32 specifically approved medical conditions in India.

articles.headline: AstraZeneca begins global withdrawal of COVID-19 vaccine Vaxzevria
articles.summary: AstraZeneca has started withdrawing its COVID-19 vaccine globally, citing a surplus of newer vaccines adapted for variants. The company stated the withdrawal is purely commercial and not related to ongoing legal cases regarding rare side effects. The vaccine was manufactured in India by Serum Institute as Covishield.

articles.headline: Karnataka mandates on-site doctors for maternity hospital blood banks
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has directed all medical institutions with blood banks to ensure the 24/7 presence of specialist doctors on-site. The move aims to prevent delays during obstetric emergencies and maternal deaths. The order follows a recent audit that identified critical gaps in maternal and newborn healthcare across the state.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry issues final draft to ban Chinese parts in drones
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence has released a final draft framework requiring over 20 mandatory lab tests to check for Chinese components in military drones. The policy aims to eliminate security vulnerabilities such as data exfiltration and GPS spoofing. Accredited labs will test hardware and software at the component level to ensure drones offered to the armed forces are genuinely indigenous.

articles.headline: CAG flags major enforcement lapses in Gujarat motor vehicle regulation
articles.summary: A CAG report tabled in the Gujarat Assembly revealed glaring lapses, including over 3,100 challans issued to car drivers for not wearing helmets. Conversely, nearly 700 bikers were penalized for seatbelt violations. The report also found 5.36 lakh vehicles operating with expired registration and nearly 70 lakh failing to renew pollution certificates, leading to massive revenue losses.

articles.headline: CBI raids Reliance Telecom offices in ₹115 crore bank fraud case
articles.summary: The CBI conducted searches at the residences of former Reliance Telecom directors Satish Seth and Gautam B Doshi, as well as company offices, over an alleged ₹114.98 crore bank fraud. The agency registered an FIR for cheating, conspiracy, and criminal misconduct. The investigation follows a complaint regarding the misuse of official positions under the Prevention of Corruption Act.

articles.headline: Women professionals challenge Karnataka's menstrual leave policy in High Court
articles.summary: Fifteen senior women professionals have petitioned the Karnataka High Court against the state's mandatory one-day paid menstrual leave policy. The petitioners label the move 'benevolent sexism' that could lead to hiring discrimination and reinforce biases. They argue that true equality requires flexible hours and universal health support rather than sex-specific mandatory leave.

articles.headline: Transgender Persons Amendment Bill triggers widespread community anxiety
articles.summary: The passage of the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill 2026 has caused an uproar in Karnataka’s trans community. Activists argue the Bill rolls back rights by removing self-identification, mandating medical board approvals, and narrowing definitions to socio-cultural identities. The community fears the law will lead to criminalization and the erasure of transmen and regional identities.

articles.headline: High Court stays government order to cancel KSOU conference
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has stayed a government directive to cancel a three-day conference titled 'Ekatma Manav Darshan' at KSOU. The court allowed the event after organizers filed an affidavit stating that no public funds were being used. The government had previously ordered the cancellation citing protocol violations and the use of university premises for a private ideological event.

articles.headline: Activists urge President to withhold assent to Transgender Amendment Bill
articles.summary: Around 140 lawyers and activists have written to President Droupadi Murmu, urging her to reject the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026. The letter cites 'constitutional violations', arguing the Bill ignores the Supreme Court's NALSA judgment on self-identification. Critics claim the mandatory medical board requirement violates privacy and bodily integrity rights.

articles.headline: Calcutta High Court cancels OBC certificates issued in Bengal after 2010
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court struck down the OBC status of several classes in West Bengal granted after 2010, affecting nearly 5 lakh certificates. The court ruled the reservations were arbitrary and bypassed the state's Backward Classes Commission. However, those who already secured jobs using these certificates will not be affected by the order.

articles.headline: SC: Vague allegations are no basis for a dowry case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that criminal proceedings for dowry harassment cannot be initiated based on vague and unsupported allegations. A bench comprising Justices BV Nagarathna and Ujjal Bhuyan quashed a case against a Kanpur-based family, noting that the allegations lacked specific details or evidence of cruelty. The court urged vigilance against the misuse of matrimonial laws.

articles.headline: Claiming 'only true religion' is an insult to other faiths: HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that claiming a particular faith is the "only true religion" constitutes an insult to other religions. Justice Srivastava made the observation while dismissing a petition seeking to quash a chargesheet against an individual accused of insulting other faiths. The court held that such remarks can disturb communal harmony.

articles.headline: UK High Court critiques Nirav Modi's reliance on ex-judges' opinions
articles.summary: A UK High Court judge has criticized fugitive diamantaire Nirav Modi for relying on the opinions of retired Indian judges to challenge his extradition. The court noted that these opinions, which alleged a lack of judicial independence in India, were "worrying" but did not provide sufficient grounds to block the extradition process. Modi remains in custody.

articles.headline: SC: Dowry deaths are a severe blot, HCs mustn't grant bail casually
articles.summary: The Supreme Court cautioned High Courts against granting bail casually in dowry death cases, describing the practice as a "severe blot on society." A bench led by Justice JK Maheshwari emphasized that courts must exercise discretion carefully and consider the gravity of the offense. The court noted that such crimes often involve extreme cruelty.

articles.headline: Nirav Modi Claims Torture Risk in UK Extradition Appeal
articles.summary: Fugitive businessman Nirav Modi told a UK court that he faces a "substantial and ongoing risk of custodial torture" if extradited to India. His legal team cited expert opinions claiming Indian prison conditions and alleged misuse of power by agencies pose a threat. The former SC judge Markandey Katju provided testimony supporting the claim of potential human rights violations.

articles.headline: Court Orders Notices to CM Siddaramaiah in MUDA Case
articles.summary: The Lokayukta in Karnataka has been ordered by a special court to issue notices to CM Siddaramaiah and others regarding a 'B report' that gave them a clean chit in the MUDA plot allotment case. The court rejected the closure report, citing a lack of evidence to substantiate the findings. This follows allegations of illegal allotment of plots in Mysuru to the CM's wife.

articles.headline: US Jury Awards $6 Million in Social Media Addiction Suit
articles.summary: A US jury in Los Angeles has awarded $6 million in damages to a 20-year-old woman in a lawsuit against Meta and Alphabet. The case alleged that the platforms' addictive features, like infinite scroll and autoplay, caused severe mental health issues. This verdict marks a significant step in holding social media companies accountable for the psychological impact of their products.

articles.headline: CJI Surya Kant urges high courts to fill vacancies with women
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant has written to the chief justices of all 25 high courts, calling for urgent action to fill 309 mounting judicial vacancies. He emphasized prioritizing the elevation of women judges as part of a push for diversity. Currently, women represent only 14.27% of working high court judges, with only one woman judge in the Supreme Court.

articles.headline: Man arrested for double murder of wife and friend in Loni
articles.summary: Ghaziabad police arrested Mohammad Rashid for the murders of his wife Shabnam and friend Faheem Ansari. The suspect was captured following a gunfight with police in which he sustained a leg injury. Rashid confessed to killing both victims following an argument using an illegal weapon he had purchased for celebratory firing at upcoming weddings.

articles.headline: Four arrested for firing at car after traffic dispute in Ghaziabad
articles.summary: Four men were arrested for firing shots at a car in Ghaziabad's Crossings Republik. The incident occurred after a traffic dispute where the suspects blocked the victim's way with an SUV. Two shots were fired at the victim, who escaped unhurt. Police seized a black Scorpio and a modified rifle, booking the suspects under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita for attempted murder.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court denies bail to man accused of minor’s rape
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court rejected a bail plea in a 2021 case involving the rape of a 13-year-old girl. The court noted the accused breached the victim's trust as she considered him a brother and had tied a rakhi on him. Justice Girish Kathpalia stated that the seriousness of the offense and the victim's corroborating testimony outweighed the fact that the accused had spent four years in custody.

articles.headline: Three minors apprehended for fatal stabbing of teenager in Delhi
articles.summary: Police have apprehended three minors, aged 13 to 17, for allegedly stabbing a 16-year-old boy to death in northeast Delhi’s Nehru Vihar. The attack was reportedly planned as retaliation for bullying. The victim was found with multiple stab wounds and declared dead at a hospital. The case is being handled under juvenile justice laws and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

articles.headline: Woman and boyfriend arrested for attempting to murder husband in Delhi
articles.summary: A 24-year-old woman and her 22-year-old boyfriend were arrested for attempting to murder her husband in Patel Nagar. The victim sustained multiple stab wounds and is currently being treated at RML Hospital. Police stated the victim is presently unfit to give a statement. The arrests followed a two-day investigation into the violent assault.

articles.headline: Notices issued to 120 people in Palam fire ministerial inquiry
articles.summary: Delhi home minister Ashish Sood has sent notices to over 120 people for statements regarding the Palam fire that killed nine people. The ministerial enquiry will record statements from eye witnesses, fire officials, and family members. A final report is expected in six weeks, focusing on safety lapses and upgrading fire infrastructure to prevent future tragedies.

articles.headline: Civil defence volunteer arrested for impersonating police officer at Tihar
articles.summary: A 30-year-old woman, Nisha Parveen, was arrested for impersonating a police officer to gain access to an inmate at Tihar Jail. She used her civil defence uniform with added badges to attempt entry into Jail No 8. Staff became suspicious of her insignia and detained her. Police suspect she intended to deliver a phone or forged medical documents to her brother, a POCSO accused.

articles.headline: Bengaluru medical professor booked for sexual harassment after classroom proposal
articles.summary: A professor at a medical college near Bengaluru was booked for sexual harassment after a video showed him proposing to a female student in class. The incident, which occurred on March 23, went viral and showed the student hitting the professor with slippers in response. Police registered the case based on a complaint from the college principal.

articles.headline: CBI arrests Mumbai man for trafficking Indians to Myanmar scam camps
articles.summary: The CBI arrested Sunil Nellathu Ramakrishnan for allegedly trafficking Indian citizens to cyber scam compounds in Myanmar’s Myawaddy region. Ramakrishnan, alias Krish, is accused of being a kingpin in a network that exploited victims in Southeast Asia. The arrest followed interviews with victims who escaped the scam camps and returned to India in 2025.

articles.headline: Special court in Odisha sentences man to death for minor’s rape
articles.summary: A fast-track Pocso court in Berhampur sentenced Keshaba Nahak to death for the rape and attempted murder of a minor girl five years ago. Nahak was convicted under the Pocso Act and various sections of the IPC. The landmark judgment followed a comprehensive trial into the 2021 incident, providing closure to the victim and her family.

articles.headline: Missing eight-year-old girl’s body found in Agra factory worker’s room
articles.summary: The body of an eight-year-old girl who went missing in Agra was found inside a flour container in a rented room occupied by a shoe factory worker. The suspect, Sunil Kumar, is currently absconding. A post-mortem identified shock and hemorrhage as the cause of death. Police have formed multiple teams to trace the suspect and are investigating if sexual assault occurred.

articles.headline: SIT probes self-styled godman Ashok Kharat for rape and extortion
articles.summary: A Special Investigation Team is investigating Nashik-based numerologist Ashok Kharat after eight FIRs were filed against him for rape and extortion. Kharat, who counseled top politicians, allegedly defrauded a Pune realtor of ₹5 crore through theatrical rituals involving snakes. The probe is also exploring Kharat's connections with high-profile state leaders.

articles.headline: Supreme Court orders SIT probe into Gujarat firm’s fraudulent insurance claim
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ordered an SIT probe into Sayona Colors Pvt Ltd for allegedly faking a fire at its godown to claim ₹28 crore in insurance. The court found the 2011 fire was an orchestrated act of arson rather than an accident. The SIT, led by a DCP-rank officer, must complete the investigation into the fraud and conspiracy within three months.

articles.headline: Allahabad High Court rules against religious disparagement in secular India
articles.summary: The Allahabad high court ruled that in secular India, no faith can claim to be the 'only true religion' as it implies disparagement of other faiths. The court dismissed a plea by a Christian priest seeking to quash criminal proceedings for allegedly criticizing other religions during prayer meetings. The judge stated such claims fall under outraging religious feelings.

articles.headline: Director Sanoj Mishra shelves film following sexual misconduct allegations
articles.summary: Filmmaker Sanoj Mishra shelved his film 'The Diary of Manipur' after actress Monalisa Bhonsle accused him of sexual misconduct while she was a minor. Mishra denied the claims and plans a defamation case, while Monalisa is reportedly seeking legal action under the POCSO Act. The controversy comes amid Monalisa's recent interfaith marriage and state elections in Kerala.

articles.headline: Bus collision and fire in Andhra Pradesh kills thirteen people
articles.summary: At least 13 people were killed and 22 injured when a private bus collided with a tipper truck in Andhra Pradesh’s Markapuram district. The bus hit the truck’s fuel tank, sparking an inferno. President Droupadi Murmu and PM Narendra Modi expressed condolences, with the Prime Minister announcing an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh for the kin of the deceased and ₹50,000 for the injured.

articles.headline: Private bus fire in Andhra Pradesh kills 14 passengers
articles.summary: Fourteen people, including six women and an infant, were charred to death after a private bus collided with a tipper lorry and caught fire near Rayavaram in Andhra Pradesh’s Markapuram district. The bus was traveling from Telangana to Nellore with 42 passengers. Preliminary reports suggest the vehicle veered into the wrong lane, though the driver claimed the steering wheel jammed.

articles.headline: Minor detained for double murder over alleged extramarital dispute
articles.summary: A 16-year-old boy was detained in northwest Bengaluru for allegedly stabbing a woman and her brother to death. The incident followed a dispute linked to an alleged extramarital relationship involving the victim's husband and the minor's mother. The victims, Yamuna and Sudeep, were attacked after confronting the woman at her residence. The case is being investigated by Byadarahalli police.

articles.headline: Medical college lecturer booked for assaulting student in Nelamangala
articles.summary: An assistant professor of microbiology at Sri Siddhartha Institute of Medical Sciences was booked under the BNS for allegedly proposing to a female student in class. The lecturer, Abdul Rahman Shariff, was reportedly assaulted by a group of students on campus following the incident. The college had previously restricted his entry following complaints of inappropriate behavior.

articles.headline: Ambulance driver booked for drunken driving on New BEL Road
articles.summary: Sadashivanagar traffic police arrested an ambulance driver, identified as Darshan, for driving under the influence of alcohol. The driver was caught operating the vehicle with sirens and lights in the wrong direction around noon on Thursday. An alcometer test confirmed alcohol consumption. No patients were inside the vehicle at the time of the incident.

articles.headline: Man detained for abusive social media posts against Bengaluru police
articles.summary: A 32-year-old man, Utkarsh Gautam, was detained for posting derogatory and abusive content against the Bengaluru City Police on X and Instagram. The accused also reportedly misbehaved with cabin crew on an IndiGo flight and attempted to open the aircraft door mid-air. He falsely claimed on social media that he was being illegally detained by airport police.

articles.headline: One more terror suspect arrested in Vijayawada for social media posts
articles.summary: The NTR Commissionerate police arrested 21-year-old Shadman Dilkash in Patna for allegedly posting terror-related content in social media groups. This is the sixth arrest in the ongoing investigation into unlawful activities. A Vijayawada court has remanded the suspect to judicial custody until April 6. Investigations into the wider network are continuing.

articles.headline: Russia to deliver remaining S-400 missile units to India by November
articles.summary: India is set to receive the final two squadrons of the S-400 air defense system from Russia this year, with one arriving in April and the last in November. Deliveries were previously delayed by the Russia-Ukraine war and supply chain issues. The expedite timeline follows high-level defense talks. India signed the $5.43 billion deal for five units in 2018.

articles.headline: Pune Porsche crash: Grandfather arrested for coercing driver to take blame
articles.summary: The Pune police arrested the grandfather of the 17-year-old involved in the fatal Porsche crash. He is accused of kidnapping and wrongfully confining the family's driver to force him to take responsibility for the accident. The minor's father remains in custody as investigators probe the swap of blood samples at Sassoon General Hospital.

articles.headline: Glitch-ridden bus hits truck, 14 burn to death in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: Fourteen people were killed and 28 injured when a private sleeper bus caught fire after hitting a tipper truck in Andhra Pradesh's Annamayya district. A technical glitch reportedly locked the sensor-operated bus doors, trapping passengers inside. Prime Minister Modi announced an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh for the kin of the deceased and ₹50,000 for the injured.

articles.headline: Heroic passenger saves lives in Andhra Pradesh bus accident
articles.summary: A passenger named Balaraju is being credited with saving several lives during the Andhra Pradesh bus crash. He managed to smash open a window and escape, then pulled several other trapped passengers to safety before the vehicle was engulfed in flames. Authorities praised his presence of mind, noting that many more would have perished otherwise.

articles.headline: PM Modi to discuss West Asia conflict preparedness with CMs
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to hold a meeting with Chief Ministers of several states to discuss India's preparedness for the ongoing West Asia conflict. The meeting will focus on energy security, the safety of Indian expats, and potential impacts on trade. The government aims to develop a coordinated strategy to mitigate regional risks.

articles.headline: Indian National Among Two Killed by Missile Debris in UAE
articles.summary: At least two people, including an Indian national, were killed in Abu Dhabi by debris from intercepted missiles launched from Iran. The UAE air defences successfully intercepted several ballistic missiles targeting civilian areas. This escalation in the regional conflict has raised serious security concerns for the large Indian diaspora residing in the United Arab Emirates.

articles.headline: Software engineer killed in Indore following dispute over penthouse rental
articles.summary: A 38-year-old Infosys software engineer, Shampa Pathak Pandey, was killed after being run over by a car in an Indore residential complex. The incident followed a heated dispute between residents and a penthouse owner over renting the property via Airbnb. Police have arrested the owner and his son for the hit-and-run after they allegedly intentionally drove into a crowd.

articles.headline: Panic buying triggers long fuel queues in multiple Indian states
articles.summary: Panic buying of fuel led to long queues at petrol pumps in Gujarat, Kashmir, Karnataka, and Goa. In Gujarat, fuel sales surged fivefold, forcing some stations to ration sales at ₹200 for bikes and ₹2,000 for cars. State governments have issued assurances of adequate supply and warned against spreading rumors on social media regarding depleted reserves.

articles.headline: Three injured in Noida house fire caused by gas leak
articles.summary: Three laborers sustained injuries after a gas leak triggered a fire at a house in Barola village, Noida. The fire broke out in the pipeline during cooking due to a damaged connection to the cylinder. Only one victim required hospital treatment for visible leg burns. Authorities confirmed there was no cylinder blast and the situation was brought under control quickly.

articles.headline: Software engineer dies by suicide in Noida Sector 78 apartment
articles.summary: A 43-year-old software engineer working for the Center of Railway Information Systems allegedly died by suicide in his Noida flat. Police recovered a suicide note requesting friends to care for his family. Originally from Bihar, the deceased lived with his wife and son. Police are currently investigating the underlying reasons for his death.

articles.headline: Delhi Police ease security restrictions in Uttam Nagar after peaceful festivals
articles.summary: Security measures in Uttam Nagar’s Hastsal Village were relaxed after Eid and Ram Navami were celebrated without incident. The locality had been heavily barricaded for three weeks following communal tensions linked to a fatal clash during Holi. While restrictions on movement were eased, senior officers stated that security personnel remain deployed to prevent any potential escalation.

articles.headline: Government grants free lifelong rail travel for Sena Medal gallantry awardees
articles.summary: Armed forces personnel awarded the Sena Medal, Nau Sena Medal, and Vayu Sena Medal for gallantry will now receive free lifelong rail travel. The benefit, which includes dependents and a companion, applies to First Class, 2AC, and chair car. Previously, this concession was limited to recipients of higher gallantry awards like the Ashok Chakra and wartime equivalents.

articles.headline: Registrar General issues strict guidelines for 2027 Census operations
articles.summary: Registrar General Mrityunjay Kumar Narayan issued a directive warning that Census officials could face three years in jail and fines for violating data collection rules. The 2027 Census begins April 1, 2026. Officials are instructed to ensure all houses are covered and to maintain polite behavior, with punitive actions prescribed for offensive questioning or damaging documents.

articles.headline: JioStar secures 27 sponsors including Google for IPL 2026
articles.summary: Official broadcaster JioStar has announced 27 sponsors for the 2026 IPL season, with Google, Campa, and Havells as co-presenting sponsors. Google Search AI will present the Match Centre Live show, marking AI as a new advertising category. Associate sponsors include AMFI, Asian Paints, and Flipkart. The network highlighted strong partner confidence despite global macroeconomic challenges.

articles.headline: IPL franchises see 30 percent growth in sponsorship revenues
articles.summary: Ahead of the 2026 season, IPL teams like Delhi Capitals and Mumbai Indians report a 20-30% growth in sponsorship revenues. Categories like BFSI, consumer tech, and renewable energy are driving diverse partnerships. Mumbai Indians have over 30 brand associations, while Punjab Kings noted longer-term deals as brands prioritize strategic integration over simple logo visibility.

articles.headline: IOC to re-introduce genetic gender testing for 2028 Olympics
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) will implement genetic testing to determine eligibility for female category events starting with the 2028 Los Angeles Games. The move marks a return to more stringent gender verification protocols. The decision follows ongoing debates surrounding fairness and inclusivity in international women's sports competitions.

articles.headline: IOC reintroduces gender tests for female category eligibility
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has announced a new eligibility policy that excludes transgender women from women's sporting events at the 2028 LA Games. The decision follows an executive order on sports and aligns with similar policies from other international sports bodies. The IOC stated that eligibility will be based on biological sex at birth.

articles.headline: IPL opening ceremony cancelled in respect for stampede victims
articles.summary: The BCCI has decided to cancel the grand opening ceremony for the 2026 IPL season. The decision was made as a mark of respect for the 11 victims who died in a recent stampede during a match at the Chinnaswamy Stadium. Instead of a ceremony, a moment of silence will be observed before the opening match between RCB and Sunrisers Hyderabad.

articles.headline: Badminton legend Carolina Marin announces retirement after knee injury
articles.summary: Spanish superstar Carolina Marin has retired at age 32, failing to recover from a third serious knee injury. Marin was the first non-Asian woman to win Olympic singles gold (Rio 2016) and won a record three world championships. Her career effectively ended during the 2024 Paris Olympics when she tore her ACL while leading a semifinal match. She was world No. 1 for 66 weeks.

articles.headline: BCCI announces full schedule for second phase of IPL 2026
articles.summary: The BCCI released fixtures for the second phase of the IPL 2026, set to run from April 13 to May 24. A total of 50 matches will be played across 12 venues, including Dharamshala and Raipur. The playoffs and final schedule will be announced later, with the championship match slated for May 31 in Bengaluru. Phase one of the tournament begins March 28.

articles.headline: Indian Grandmaster Praggnanandhaa prepares for second Candidates tournament
articles.summary: R Praggnanandhaa will participate in his second Candidates tournament in Cyprus starting March 29. The 20-year-old world number four has focused on physical endurance and tactical aggression with coach RB Ramesh after a tiring 2025 schedule. Pragg aims to improve on his 2024 fifth-place finish and secure a shot at the World Chess Championship.

articles.headline: Germany’s Freddy Schott takes early lead at Indian Open golf
articles.summary: Freddy Schott carded a 6-under 66 to lead the Hero Indian Open at Gurugram's DLF Golf course. Spain’s Eugenio Chacarra is one shot behind after a 5-under 67. Tournament favorite Akshay Bhatia struggled with an 8-over front nine, eventually finishing 5-over. Veer Ahlawat is currently the leading Indian after an even-par 72 in the first round.

articles.headline: Doping case of quarter-miler Sneha Kolleri raises questions for NDTL
articles.summary: Quarter-miler Sneha Kolleri received a three-year ban after the Paris lab detected stanozolol in a sample previously cleared as negative by India’s NDTL. The failure of the national lab to identify the steroid metabolites led to international embarrassment at the World Athletics Relays. The case has prompted calls for upgrading NDTL’s technology to match global sensitivity levels.

articles.headline: Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra to begin training camp in Turkey
articles.summary: Neeraj Chopra will move his training to Antalya, Turkey, for 55 days starting April 1 to prepare for the Asian and Commonwealth Games. The Mission Olympic Cell approved ₹35.8 lakh for the camp. Chopra, joined by physio Ishaan Marwaha and coach Jai Choudhary, aims to open his season at the Doha Diamond League if regional conflict permits the event to proceed.

articles.headline: IOC bans transgender women from competing in female Olympic categories
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee approved a new policy excluding transgender women from women's Olympic events. Effective from the 2028 Los Angeles Games, eligibility will be limited to biological females determined by a mandatory gene test. The IOC stated the move protects fairness and safety in female categories, aligning with recent international sporting trends.

articles.headline: BCCI announces venues for 2027 Border-Gavaskar Trophy Test series
articles.summary: India will host Australia for the five-match Border-Gavaskar Trophy from January 21 to March 7, 2027. Tests will be played in Nagpur, Chennai, Guwahati, Ranchi, and Ahmedabad. The upcoming home season will also feature series against West Indies and Sri Lanka, totaling 22 international matches across 17 venues, including first-time hosts like New Chandigarh.

articles.headline: RBI approves record ₹2.11 lakh crore dividend for fiscal year 2024
articles.summary: The RBI board approved a record ₹2.11 lakh crore surplus transfer to the government for FY24, far exceeding the ₹1.02 lakh crore budget estimate and the previous year's ₹87,416 crore. This fiscal windfall is expected to help the government reduce its fiscal deficit and boost capital expenditure on infrastructure projects ahead of the final budget for the current financial year.

articles.headline: Private refiner Nayara Energy hikes petrol and diesel prices
articles.summary: Private fuel retailer Nayara Energy increased petrol prices by ₹5 and diesel by ₹3 per litre, driven by rising Brent crude prices reaching $107 per barrel due to the West Asia conflict. While state-run oil marketing companies have kept rates frozen despite estimated losses of ₹25 per litre, Nayara is the first to revise rates during the current geopolitical crisis.

articles.headline: Nokia restructures India leadership with new Country Manager appointments
articles.summary: Nokia has revamped its Indian leadership effective April 1, 2026. Samar Mittal has been appointed India Country Business Leader to oversee go-to-market strategies for telecom, cloud, and AI. Vibha Mehra, formerly of Microsoft and Intel with 26 years of experience, becomes India Country Manager, leading government relations, communications, and CSR initiatives.

articles.headline: Amit Harlalka appointed new CEO of ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel India
articles.summary: ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel India has named current CFO Amit Harlalka as its new CEO, effective July 1, 2026. He succeeds Dilip Oommen, who is retiring after 22 years. Oommen will remain a board member and Vice Chairman of ArcelorMittal India to ensure a smooth transition. Harlalka joined the group in 2019 and has held various international roles within the company.

articles.headline: Infosys to acquire two healthcare and insurance firms for ₹5,260 crore
articles.summary: Infosys will acquire U.S.-based Optimum Healthcare IT for $465 million and insurance consulting firm Stratus in deals totaling approximately ₹5,260 crore. Expected to close by Q1 FY2027, the acquisitions aim to strengthen Infosys’s digital transformation and global delivery capabilities within the healthcare and insurance sectors, adding specialized industry knowledge to its portfolio.

articles.headline: Allianz-Jio Reinsurance begins operations with IRDAI approval
articles.summary: Allianz-Jio Reinsurance Limited, a 50-50 joint venture between Jio Financial Services and Allianz Group, has commenced operations in Mumbai. Led by CEO Sonia Rawal, the firm received final IRDAI approval on March 12, 2026. The JV combines Jio’s digital infrastructure with Allianz’s global expertise to provide reinsurance capacity and support India’s 'Insurance for All' 2047 goal.

articles.headline: Jaishankar arrives in France for G7 foreign ministers’ meeting
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar arrived in France for the G7 Foreign Ministers' meeting to discuss the West Asia conflict, Ukraine, and maritime security. He met French counterpart Jean-Noël Barrot regarding the reopening of shipping routes and the Strait of Hormuz. Prime Minister Modi has confirmed his attendance at the main G7 summit in Evian scheduled for June 15-17.

articles.headline: PM Modi to review West Asia conflict preparedness with Chief Ministers
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will hold a virtual meeting with Chief Ministers and Secretaries of non-poll-bound states on Friday to review India's preparedness for the West Asia conflict. Focus areas include fuel supply, inflationary pressure, and maritime security as global oil prices surge past $105. PM Modi urged a collaborative 'Team India' approach to prevent profiteering.

articles.headline: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan calls Congress the ‘B-team’ of BJP
articles.summary: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan labeled the Congress as the 'B-team' of the BJP, accusing Rahul Gandhi of lacking political understanding and weakening opposition unity. Vijayan claimed Congress actions have indirectly helped the BJP gain power and cited Arvind Kejriwal's acquittal as proof of politically motivated actions. The attack follows Gandhi's claims of collusion between the LDF and BJP.

articles.headline: Karnataka Speaker directs government to ensure VIP IPL tickets for MLAs
articles.summary: Karnataka Assembly Speaker U.T. Khader has directed the state government to ensure each MLA is allotted four VIP tickets for IPL matches at Bengaluru's M. Chinnaswamy Stadium. The directive follows complaints from legislators about being denied entry or forced to stand in queues. Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar agreed to discuss the matter with the state cricket association.

articles.headline: PM Narendra Modi to inaugurate first phase of Jewar International Airport
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the first phase of the Noida International Airport in Jewar on Saturday. Developed at a cost of ₹11,200 crore under a PPP model, the facility initially handles 12 million passengers annually with a 3,900-metre runway. It is expected to eventually serve 70 million passengers, easing traffic at Delhi's IGIA and boosting regional logistics.

articles.headline: UGC permits universities to offer biannual admissions from 2024-25 session
articles.summary: The University Grants Commission has approved a proposal allowing higher education institutions to admit students twice a year, in July-August and January-February, starting from the 2024-25 session. This flexibility aims to assist students who miss traditional admission cycles and helps align Indian universities with international academic calendars and standards.

articles.headline: Indian and Pakistani killed by intercepted missile debris in UAE
articles.summary: An Indian and a Pakistani national were killed in Abu Dhabi when debris from ballistic missiles intercepted by UAE air defenses fell on a city street. Three others, including an Indian, were injured. This marks the first Indian fatality in the UAE since the West Asia conflict began on February 28. UAE defenses have intercepted 372 ballistic and 15 cruise missiles during the conflict.

articles.headline: Nepal commission seeks prosecution of K.P. Sharma Oli over protest deaths
articles.summary: An investigative panel in Nepal has recommended prosecuting former PM K.P. Sharma Oli for negligence and 'reckless killings' during anti-corruption Gen Z protests in September. The report alleges Oli failed to prevent lethal police firing that contributed to 76 total deaths. The recommendations could lead to up to 10 years in prison for Oli, whose resignation was forced by the unrest.

articles.headline: First International Film Festival Delhi opens at Bharat Mandapam
articles.summary: The inaugural International Film Festival Delhi (IFFD) 2026, launched by CM Rekha Gupta, is running at Bharat Mandapam until March 31. The event features global screenings and an archival exhibition curated by Neville Tuli, showcasing hand-painted posters and memorabilia from classics like 'Sholay' and 'Mera Naam Joker,' alongside sections dedicated to cinema legends like Dharmendra.

articles.headline: Karnataka High Court issues notice to CM Siddaramaiah in MUDA case
articles.summary: The High Court of Karnataka has issued notices to CM Siddaramaiah and his wife Parvathi regarding the MUDA site allotment case. The petition, filed by Snehamayi Krishna, challenges a Lokayukta 'B' report that closed the investigation into the allotment of 14 residential sites. The petitioner alleges the probe was biased and seeks a fresh investigation by an independent agency.

articles.headline: Ten killed in Madhya Pradesh bus-truck collision in Chhindwara
articles.summary: Ten people were killed and 35 injured when a private bus, returning from a government event, collided with a truck and overturned in Chhindwara, Madhya Pradesh. The accident occurred while the bus was attempting to overtake. Chief Minister Mohan Yadav, who attended the earlier function, expressed grief and announced free medical treatment for the survivors.

articles.headline: Suspected Kuki militants fire at Army post in Manipur's Bishnupur
articles.summary: Suspected Kuki militants attacked an Indian Army post in Manipur’s Bishnupur district, leading to a 30-minute gun battle with security forces including the CRPF and local police. No casualties were reported. Following the incident, security forces launched extensive search operations in the nearby hills to flush out insurgents and have increased surveillance in the area.

articles.headline: 14 killed in Andhra Pradesh bus-truck collision and fire
articles.summary: Fourteen people died and 27 were injured after a private bus traveling from Jagtial to Kaligiri collided with a gravel-laden tipper truck in Rayavaram, Andhra Pradesh. The collision triggered a massive fire that gutted both vehicles. Preliminary reports suggest the bus steering jammed, causing the driver to lose control. The state has announced ex gratia payments of ₹2 lakh to ₹5 lakh.

articles.headline: KKR enters IPL 2024 final with dominant win over SRH
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders became the first team to reach the IPL 2024 final after a dominant eight-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad in Qualifier 1 at Ahmedabad. Mitchell Starc’s three-wicket haul restricted SRH to 159, while half-centuries from Venkatesh and Shreyas Iyer secured the chase. SRH will now face the winner of the Eliminator for a second chance to reach the final.
